questions.question: Which part of the Central Nervous System controls “reflex Actions” ?
questions.explanation: The nervous system is made of two parts viz. Central Nervous System (CNS) and the Peripheral Nervous System (PNS). CNS is made of Brain [cerebrum, brainstem and cerebellum] and spinal cord. The PNS is made of nerves and neural ganglia. Further, the meninges (three membranes that envelop the brain and spinal cord) are also a part of the nervous system

questions.question: What are the two categories of cell which nervous system is made up of__?

options.label: D
options.text: Tendon cell and Macula densa cell

questions.explanation: The Nervous system is made up of two types of cells: Glia, which regulate homeostasis, providing support and protection to the function of neurons and Neurons, which transmit signals between them and from one part of the body to another.

questions.question: Corpus Callosum makes an important part of which among the following organs in Human body?
questions.explanation: The corpus callosum is a large, C-shaped nerve fiber bundle which is found beneath the cerebral cortex. It stretches across the midline of the brain. IT connects the left and right cerebral hemispheres.

questions.question: Which among the following is not a function of Sympathetic nerves in Autonomic nervous system?
questions.explanation: contract urinary bladder is a function of Parasympathetic nerves. Sympathetic nerves relax urinary bladder.

questions.question: The foramen of Monro is associated with _?
questions.explanation: The interventricular foramen is also known as foramen of Monro. It is a part of the ventricular system and the connection between the third ventricle and the lateral ventricles.
